strengths: The problem motivation is clear and well-articulated: the physical failure of Lambertian/EPI assumptions on specular and translucent surfaces is a meaningful and underexplored research gap in light field depth estimation.

The three-layer angular signal decomposition (DC + linear + residual) provides an interpretable physical basis for material classification, which is more principled than purely data-driven approaches.
The writing in the abstract and introduction is generally fluent and structured, with explicit numerical claims (MAE=0.411 failure boundary) that frame a concrete research contribution.
weaknesses: CRITICAL: Citation keys are blatantly mismatched to described content. \cite{he2016} (likely ResNet) is cited for light field imaging; \cite{vaswani2017} (Transformer) for downstream applications; \cite{ronneberger2015} (U-Net) for Williem et al.'s LF stereo matching; \cite{dosovitskiy2021} (ViT) for Shin et al.'s EPINET; \cite{blinn1977} for Wanner & Goldluecke. This suggests placeholder/recycled bibliography and raises serious integrity concerns. Only 5 unique citations are visible â€” far below the expected 50+ for TCSVT.

Severe structural duplication: related work content (traditional methods, deep learning methods, non-Lambertian methods) appears twice â€” once embedded before Section II declaration and again as empty subsection stubs under \section{Related Work}. This indicates an incomplete draft merge.

No experimental evidence is visible in the truncated 24K-character source. No \includegraphics commands, no result tables, no figure environments with actual data. The abstract claims 'extensive experiments' and specific MAE values but the source contains zero supporting figures or tables. The \markboth header is truncated to 'Non-Lambertia' â€” a sloppy formatting error.
key_gaps_vs_tcsvt: Citation integrity failure: Mismatched citation keys (he2016â†’LF imaging, ronneberger2015â†’Williem et al., dosovitskiy2021â†’Shin et al.) indicate either placeholder bibliography or academic carelessness. TCSVT requires accurate, complete referencing (typically 40-60+ citations for full-length papers). Only 5 visible citations is grossly insufficient.

Structural incompleteness: The paper has duplicate related-work sections (content before Section II + empty stubs under Section II), a truncated \markboth header, and no visible experimental section within 24K characters. This suggests the manuscript was not ready for submission.

Insufficient experimental substantiation: Despite claims of '130+ experimental trials across 16 directions,' no figures, tables, ablation studies, or BadPix metrics are present in the source. TCSVT requires comprehensive empirical validation across multiple datasets with SOTA comparisons â€” none is visible here.
